questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort algorithm?
questions.explanation: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The pass through the list is repeated until the list is sorted. The time complexity of bubble sort is O(n^2) in the worst and average cases, and O(n) in the best case.

questions.question: Which of the following is a built-in sorting algorithm in Python?
questions.explanation: Insertion sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is inserted into the sorted region at its correct position.

questions.question: What is the advantage of Insertion Sort over Bubble Sort?
options: More efficient for reverse sorted lists
questions.explanation: Insertion sort is more efficient for reverse sorted lists because it only requires a single pass through the list to identify the largest element and move it to the end.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of Selection Sort?
options: It is a stable sorting algorithm
It is an in-place sorting algorithm
It is a comparison-based sorting algorithm
It is slower than Insertion Sort
questions.explanation: Selection sort can be slower than insertion sort for large lists because it requires finding the minimum element in the unsorted region, which can be time-consuming for large datasets.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort algorithm?
questions.explanation: Selection sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is selected and swapped with the first element of the sorted region. The time complexity of selection sort is O(n^2) in the worst and average cases, and O(n) in the best case.
